module_mood_contract: Practitioner-widening — student sees where M6 loop skills project to org-scale CI/CD; recognition and mild urgency, not anxiety.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named; every teaching beat holds floor.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All teaching phases at 8+/10 except Phase 6 (CTO Monday checklist) at 7/10. Phase 6 is a TEACHING beat (the seven-question diagnostic is pedagogy). 7 = facilitator-premium territory per judge template — not REVISE, but flagged. Phases 1-5 and 7 all 8+/10. Close at 8/10.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Phase 6 would reach 8/10 if the seven-question frame acknowledged the IC-proxy use: 'these are the questions you can bring to your tech lead or answer for your own lane' — the audience declaration says staff engineers, but the body reads as pure CTO monologue in that section.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood floor applies to TEACHING beats only — setup/launch beats exempt.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All seven phases are reading/comprehension beats; no setup or launch beats present. Mood floor correctly applies to all phases. Carve-out not needed.

Entire piece is epistemic — 'these are the forces, here is the move.' No failure-attribution framing. Theatre gates line ('Theatre gates slow throughput without catching anything') is systemic critique, not shame directed at the reader.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Opens with 'systems-throughput question' (a discipline frame). Six forces each name a design discipline (blast-radius stratification, eval-of-evals management, environmental parity, attribution clarity). No failure-word openers.

Strategy doc (item #5, Supplementary material planned) specifies: six forces named, human-review-as-design-review reframe, fast-forward-requires-fast-reverse pace frame, three anchor cases (Intercom / Ramp / Klaassen), CTO Monday checklist. All five present in the file. The specific anchor cases and force names match the strategy spec verbatim.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No authored conversations, no pause-cue sentences, no 'discuss with...' or 'share with your neighbor' beats. Piece is pure reference prose — all seven sections are about the work (the CI/CD system, the forces, the moves). N/A for cohort-management authoring.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Supplementary reference document — no fenced prompt blocks. Students read and reference; they do not copy-paste prompts from this file. Rule does not fire.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lecture placement — meta-frame lectures are closers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Supplementary reference doc, not a lecture. Placement rules for lectures do not apply.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands across persona variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Three core teaching moments — 'design review not line review' (Phase 3, mood 9/10), 'fast forward requires fast reverse investment flip' (Phase 4), 'six-force taxonomy as vocabulary' (Phase 2) — all land in the persona trace. A fast-operator persona would skim anchor cases but still absorb the three frames. An opinionated-senior persona would push back on the single-source Intercom numbers (maintainer block already flags this). Teaching moments are in body prose and section framing, not in prompts, so they're structurally reachable by all persona types.

Orphan supplementary — not linked from a module yet. No cross-module mood arc to protect. The piece's own close ('Where this fits in the AE101 arc') points forward correctly to org-scale from M6; it does not resolve the M6 mood prematurely since it is designed to sit after M6.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity to AE101 strategy.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc Big Idea for this piece: 'evals-as-infrastructure at PR/CI gate scale — where the M6 skills go next.' File opens with the throughput-vs-review-capacity gap, runs through six forces, closes with arc placement. Fidelity is tight: the file is the strategy item rendered as prose, not a reinterpretation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — handoff to next module open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Supplementary orphan — not yet linked from a module. Arc handoff rule does not apply. When linked from M6, the existing arc close ('Once you own the loop at your own desk, the next gate up is the one where your agent-drafted PR meets the rest of the org') is the correct bridge.

stealing_the_mood: Phase 6 (CTO Monday checklist) — 7/10. Teaching beat; mood floor applies. The seven-question frame is unmistakably CTO-monologue and the IC/staff-engineer persona feels slightly distanced. Would reach 8/10 with one sentence acknowledging the IC-proxy use of the checklist. Not blocking (7 is facilitator-premium, not below-7 REVISE threshold).
